f_category: Women's ethnic wear — bandhej, kurtis, Anarkalis, gowns & lehengas
f_brief: Jodhpur-born ethnic wear brand (2019, Nai Sarak — Rajasthan's largest ethnic outlet). Three stores today (Jodhpur ×2 + Ahmedabad), launching two new stores — one more in Ahmedabad, second city to be confirmed. Wants styling-led content instead of plain product posts, and wants to speak to Gujarati and Rajasthani audiences in their own language. Strong Instagram base (137K) to build on. Scope: social media, influencer & UGC, PR around the store launches.

heard: Jodhpur-born ethnic-wear brand — started 2019 at Nai Sarak, Rajasthan's largest ethnic outlet
Three stores running (Jodhpur Nai Sarak, Sardarpura + Ahmedabad); two new stores launching — one in Ahmedabad, second city TBC
Wants styling-led content — lookbooks, drape & styling reels, not just product posts
Core audiences: Gujarati & Rajasthani women — content should speak their language
Already has a 137K-follower Instagram base and a Shopify store shipping to US & Canada
Scope: social media + influencer & UGC + PR around the new store launches
research.mktlabel: India ethnic wear market, 2023 — headed to $30.4B by 2030 (Grand View Research)

personas.d: Shopping the full occasion wardrobe — lehengas, gowns, family sets. Decides in-store; discovers on Instagram.
Navratri, Diwali, weddings every season. Saves styling reels, wants 'how do I wear this' content in her language.
Heritage-proud, buys bandhej online for festivals abroad — the Shopify store already ships to her.

competitors.g: National scale, mall presence, big festive campaigns
competitors.b: Mass-produced feel — no regional bandhej authenticity
Thin offline presence, generic designs, no heritage story
Weaker in West India, little creator-led content
Heavy bridal price point, dated digital & social presence

doingwell: Big festive campaign spends & celebrity faces
gaps: Regional heritage (bandhej) told generically — no authentic Rajasthan-Gujarat voice
Almost no styling / how-to-wear content — feeds are catalogues
UGC & community rarely activated — customers never become the content
Store launches happen quietly — no PR moments
advantage: Own the bandhej heritage story — a real Jodhpur landmark, not a label
Styling-first content engine: lookbooks, drape reels, 'one kurti, three ways'
Speak Gujarati & Marwari — vernacular reels and voiceovers the majors won't do
Turn 3→5 store launches into PR + influencer moments
Make customers the models — try-on UGC from the stores every week
research.positioning: "Bandhani — Rajasthan's ethnic-wear landmark, now styling Gujarat. Heritage bandhej, styled for today."

pillars.d: Drape reels, 'style it three ways', festive looks — the content she saves.
Bandhej stories, craftsmen, Jodhpur roots — the trust the majors can't copy.
Navratri, Diwali, shaadi-season capsules dropped like collections.
Customer try-ons, UGC features, store vlogs and launch events.

research.webaudit: Shopify store is live with US & Canada shipping — but thin: no styling content or lookbooks, no reviews, minimal brand story, pricing hidden on landing. Quick wins: styling galleries, store pages with maps, festive edits, review capture.
research.socialaudit: @bandhani_officeal has a strong 137K base with 3.2K posts — but the feed is product-catalogue heavy. Shift to styling reels, vernacular voiceovers, store vlogs and a UGC hashtag; the audience is already there, the format isn't.
research.sentsummary: A known Jodhpur landmark with warm local press (CityShor coverage) and loyal store footfall; online reviews are sparse and the digital story lags the offline reputation — a perception gap content can close fast.

gtm.d: Content system + templates, styling shoots, vernacular formats, profile revamp.
Ahmedabad store #2 + new city as PR + creator events — openings become campaigns.
UGC hashtag, customer features, try-on reels, WhatsApp broadcast & loyalty.
Navratri–Diwali–shaadi season blitz; diaspora push on the Shopify store.

funnel.l: Styling reels + Gujarati/Rajasthani creators reach her feed
Lookbooks, saves, WhatsApp enquiries, store-visit intent
Community features, festive drops, repeat occasions
